# Questions in Livestream 184

Live Stream #184: Priesthood of the Most Sacred Question

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GhhX7GR-zvE?t=1207" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">20:07</a> | Is Revelation future or past, was Nero the Antichrist, and what is his view of the Rapture? | He gives short answers: he is futurist on Revelation, rejects Nero as the Antichrist, and keeps the Rapture discussion brief. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GhhX7GR-zvE?t=1422" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">23:42</a> | How should 1 John 2:2 be read when it says Christ is the propitiation not only for our sins but for the whole world? | He treated it as a debated text, but argued it should not be read as an uncomplicated proof of unlimited atonement. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GhhX7GR-zvE?t=2077" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">34:37</a> | Why do many devout Christian young adults reject the faith at university, and what can churches do? | He points to young-adult peer-group pressures and says churches need to prepare youth before that developmental stage. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GhhX7GR-zvE?t=2939" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">48:59</a> | Does forgiving others mean not demanding restitution or damages after a crime? | He says forgiveness and restitution are related but distinct, so pursuing lawful restitution need not violate forgiveness. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GhhX7GR-zvE?t=4499" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:14:59</a> | Is there a connection between Genesis 33:10 and Genesis 41:45? | He says the question would need to be more specific, then reads the passages and gives only a limited answer. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GhhX7GR-zvE?t=4587" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:16:27</a> | Where in the ancient Near East is blood described as black? | He points to Semitic literature where spilled blood on the ground can be described as black. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GhhX7GR-zvE?t=4798" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:19:58</a> | When did Norse and Greek myths develop? | He says he does not know, since Greek and Scandinavian myth are outside his area. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GhhX7GR-zvE?t=5013" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:23:33</a> | What is your opinion of the Megiddo Mosaic? | He called it an important early Christian discovery that helps undercut the claim that Jesus was only treated as divine much later. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GhhX7GR-zvE?t=5507" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:31:47</a> | Was the Aswan Dam a big mistake? | He says yes and no, noting both benefits and serious consequences such as loss of Nile silt. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GhhX7GR-zvE?t=6030" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:40:30</a> | What do we know about Edom's god Qos? | He says almost nothing is known, apart from limited attestations such as seals and non-religious inscriptions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GhhX7GR-zvE?t=6475" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:47:55</a> | What are his feelings about buying small antiquities online? | He advises caution because authenticity and looting history are uncertain, and legal purchase usually depends on pre-1970 provenance. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GhhX7GR-zvE?t=6982" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:56:22</a> | How did deportation work in ancient Egypt? | He says formal deportation was not typical; Egypt more often excluded, expelled, punished, or executed people. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GhhX7GR-zvE?t=7052" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:57:32</a> | Is this the layout of the Exodus crossing, with Israelites entering Sinai through dry land? | He says the Israelites crossed dry land but corrects parts of the proposed route and sequence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GhhX7GR-zvE?t=7293" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:01:33</a> | What was manna, and can we find something like it today? | He says the word itself means something like "what is this," then discusses possible natural analogues cautiously. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GhhX7GR-zvE?t=8047" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:14:07</a> | Was the man ever named Adam in Genesis 2-3? | He says the text mainly calls him "the man," with the definite noun functioning like a designation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GhhX7GR-zvE?t=8200" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:16:40</a> | What were the repercussions when elites killed peasants or lower-class people in ancient Egypt? | He says it was against the law even for elites or Pharaoh to kill arbitrarily, though abuses could happen. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GhhX7GR-zvE?t=8921" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:28:41</a> | How is HALOT laid out: dictionary order or conjugations? | He says HALOT is a lexicon laid out in Hebrew alphabetic order, not by conjugation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GhhX7GR-zvE?t=9131" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:32:11</a> | How much support did non-Trinitarian Arian belief really have around Nicaea? | He said very little among the bishops themselves, though it later had more traction among laypeople and some post-Roman groups. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GhhX7GR-zvE?t=9236" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:33:56</a> | What are his thoughts about feelings versus reasoning in scripture and belief? | He says reasoning should channel feelings, and both mind and affections need to be submitted to Christ. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GhhX7GR-zvE?t=11692" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:14:52</a> | Is the land in Genesis 2:13 better read as Kish or Gish rather than Kush? | He argues for Gish/Kish in Mesopotamia and says the surrounding geography fits Mesopotamian locations. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GhhX7GR-zvE?t=13070" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:37:50</a> | Could Jude 6 refer to the angels who sinned? | He says Jude must be read as a structured passage and likely refers to angels who rebelled by dereliction of duty. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GhhX7GR-zvE?t=14176" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:56:16</a> | How many languages can he read, and what advice does he have for learning languages? | He says he reads about 14 languages and recommends focusing on one beginner language for a year before layering in another. |
